title: A Day of Fallen Night
author: Samantha Shannon
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "A Day of Fallen Night" book by Samantha Shannon?
Options:
A. Furtia and I intend to free her from Taugran. Taugran is much mightier than Furtia, Nikeya objected. You know this is true, Dumai. Not for long. I feel the star's coming. She was right. Her sense of its approach was intense and taut, like a lute string pulled tight along the neck, tuned to something nearing by the hour. Nikeya, Unora said gently, touching her shoulder, would you give us a moment alone, and watch over Epabo? Certainly. Nikeya looked worriedly at Dumai and went back into the tent. Unora held Dumai's cheek. Do not travel to Muysima. I must, though I wish it were not so, Dumai.
B. Furtia and I will free her from captivity. Taugran has far greater strength than Furtia, Nikeya protested. You know this is true, Dumai. Soon that will change. I sense the star's imminent arrival. She was right. Her awareness of its approach was intense and powerful, like a string stretched taut along a lute's neck, attuned to something drawing closer by the hour. Nikeya, Unora murmured gently, putting her hand on her shoulder, would you leave us for a moment, and keep watch over Epabo? Of course. Nikeya gave Dumai a worried look and went back into the tent. Unora cupped Dumai's cheek in her hand. Do not make the journey to Muysima. I have no choice, I wish it were different, Dumai.  
C. Furtia and I will get her away. Taugran is much stronger than Furtia, Nikeya protested. You know this, Dumai. Not for long. I feel the coming of the star. It was true. Her awareness of it was taut and strong, a string pulled along the neck of a lute, tuned to something that moved closer by the hour. Nikeya, Unora said quietly, touching her shoulder, will you leave us a moment, and watch over Epabo? Of course. Nikeya dealt Dumai a concerned look and went back to the tent. Unora took Dumai by the cheek. Do not go to Muysima. I have to I wished, Dumai.
D. Furtia and I intend to rescue her. Taugran is much more powerful than Furtia, Nikeya objected. You understand this, Dumai. Not for much longer. I sense the approaching star. It was accurate. Her consciousness of it was taut and robust, a chord pulled along a lute's neck, tuned to something drawing nearer by the hour. Nikeya, Unora uttered softly, touching her shoulder, could you give us a minute, and keep an eye on Epabo? Absolutely. Nikeya looked at Dumai with concern and returned to the tent. Unora held Dumai's cheek. Do not travel to Muysima. I must go, I wished, Dumai.
